message: Node started: Derive from first principles the probability that two random chords intersect, given that their endpoints must lie in different quadrants. First, define the sample space of quadrant pairs for two chords (Chord A and Chord B). Since each chord has two endpoints in different quadrants, identify all possible pairs of quadrants for Chord A and Chord B. For each pair of chords, calculate the probability of intersection by considering the relative order of the four endpoints on the circle. Explicitly enumerate all valid quadrant combinations (e.g., Chord A in quadrants 1 and 2, Chord B in quadrants 2 and 3) and determine the intersection probability for each. Finally, calculate the weighted average probability across all possible quadrant assignments to find the total probability P that any two such chords intersect.
